title: GROUP_ADD: is_group_member check not atomic with add_group_member_capped
description: ws.rs:1454 — is_group_member() and add_group_member_capped() are separate DB calls with no shared transaction. A concurrent GROUP_LEAVE from the same client between these two calls causes: (1) is_group_member returns true, (2) the client leaves, (3) add_group_member_capped runs as if caller is still a member, so a former member adds another user to a group they have left. Fix: fold the caller-is-member check into the same transaction as add_group_member_capped, or accept the race as a minor invariant violation with no security impact (adding someone to a group you just left is harmless).
